markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify SQLite cannot appear in touched storage, cache, tests, fixtures, examples, or temp adapters. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: NoSQLiteTripwireFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: NoSQLiteTripwireFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-225..229 add real secret/path leakage negative fixtures, portable path redaction/validation including wrappers and Windows drive roots, Postgres-only storage fail-closed behavior, Node/Rust forbidden-source tripwires, actual allowlist authority, and fail-closed scanner read-error handling.

commands.result: PASS; 3/3 source tripwire unit tests passed
PASS; 9/9 dependency policy checks passed, forbidden-source-tripwire clean

evidence.summary: Reviewer attacked detector drift, wrapper/delimiter path leaks, bare drive roots, UNC/POSIX paths, Node/Rust tripwire split-brain, broad source exceptions, read-error skipping, path-only probes, SQLite fallback/default behavior, and mock-only proof; no blockers found.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-228 requires a real no-SQLite tripwire fixture proving SQLite cannot appear in storage/cache/tests/fixtures/examples/temp adapters, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger as canonical authority and no SQLite fallback. The implementation delivers a real source-pattern scanner, a real allowlist forbidden-class declaration, fail-closed PostgreSQL-only storage config, and negative-fixture tests that exercise the real Handshake-native scanner, allowlist, and Node validator subprocess. Aligns exactly with current Master Spec v02.194 which forbids SQLite and names SqlitePool as strictly forbidden.
findings: source_tripwires.rs:21-94 declares real BUILT_IN_PATTERNS for the sqlite class (sqlite://, .sqlite3, rusqlite, sqlx::Sqlite, SqlitePool, SqlitePoolOptions, SqliteConnectOptions, SqliteDatabase, 'sqlite fallback') plus outside_app/outside_server_daemon patterns; scan_source_text (222-257) matches both path and content, fail-closed.

app/src/lib/dependency_policy/runtime_dependency_allowlist.json:64-68 declares the real sqlite forbidden class with cargo_crate_name_substrings ['sqlite'], npm_package_names, and full source_scan_patterns; product_scan_roots cover app/src + handshake_core/src + src-tauri/src; source_tripwire_exceptions (181+) are exact path+pattern scoped to negative-assertion test files, keeping the production tripwire active elsewhere.

tests/security_portability_validation_tests.rs:404-447 (mt_228_no_sqlite_tripwire_rejects_storage_cache_fixture_and_temp_adapter_refs) asserts sqlite-class violations for 7 storage/cache/fixture/example/temp-adapter fixtures using the real loaded allowlist; lines 450-477 assert the allowlist declares required patterns; lines 529-565 write a real temp .rs file under Handshake_Artifacts and assert the file-scoped policy gate fails closed reporting sqlite/SqlitePool.

tests/security_portability_validation_tests.rs:567-699 spawn the real Node validator (app/scripts/check-dependency-policy.mjs --source-tripwire-files) as a subprocess and assert non-zero exit with file_read_errors / forbidden-source-tripwire / path-name scanning; check-dependency-policy.mjs:67,168,198-229 genuinely implement --source-tripwire-files, read errors, and process.exitCode=1 on failure — not a no-op.

storage/mod.rs:186-217 ControlPlaneStorageConfig::resolve is genuinely fail-closed: PostgresPrimary is the only supported ControlPlaneStorageMode (from_str maps everything else to 'unsupported storage mode' at 162), DATABASE_URL must pass is_postgres_url (224-226); a sqlite:// URL or sqlite mode is rejected and NO SQLite fallback branch exists — directly substantiating MT-228's no-fallback claim and verified by mt_227 test (377-402).

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2304-2308 forbids SQLite syntax/shims and 2388 declares 'fn sqlite_pool(&self) -> Option<&SqlitePool> is strictly FORBIDDEN' — the exact pattern this tripwire detects; preamble v02.184 records no-SQLite Kernel V1 authority law. No spec drift.

Sub-rule 1: grep of the diff for the touched files found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/stub/placeholder/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable escapes reachable from the tripwire path.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): lifecycle.implementation_commit 9915fcfa bundles MT-225..229; completed_by is set with claimed_by null (no same-actor self-cert handoff malformation), consistent with winding-down repo governance and not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
